meta.title: Vaerum: Heirs of the Ether
meta.description: A digital card table for friends. Move and flip cards freely; follow the official rulebook yourselves.

ui.deckReset: The deck has been gathered and shuffled.
ui.joinAria: Enter a room code to join
ui.newRoom: A new room has been opened.
ui.roomFull: The room is full, so you are watching as a spectator.
ui.pasteJoin: Paste code or link to join
ui.invalidCode: No valid room code on the clipboard

diag.title: Connection self-test
diag.subtitle: Checks whether multiplayer sync is set up correctly.
diag.verdictOk: All good. Live multiplayer sync is working.
diag.verdictBad: Multiplayer sync is not working yet. See the note below.
diag.error: The test could not run. Please try again.
diag.hint_config-missing: Set SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY in your hosting environment (Vercel), then redeploy. You can verify them by opening /api/config in your browser; both values must be filled in.
diag.hint_url-bad: The project address must be exactly https://your-project.supabase.co, with no extra path. Copy it from your Supabase dashboard under Project Settings, Data API.
diag.hint_rest-failed: The project could not be reached, or the anon key was rejected. Confirm the URL and the anon (public) key, and make sure the Supabase project is not paused.
diag.hint_realtime-failed: The project is reachable but the Realtime socket did not connect. In your Supabase dashboard, make sure Realtime is enabled, and check that no firewall or proxy is blocking the websocket.

kick.title: Remove player
kick.body: Remove {name} from the table? They will be sent to a new, empty room.
kick.kicked: The host removed you from the table.

shortcutsList.title: Shortcuts
shortcutsList.subtitle: The cursor target wins; empty space does nothing.

items.desc: Move the card under the cursor
Move the whole stack under the cursor
Flip the stack under the cursor (a single card flips alone)
Flip the single card under the cursor
Flip the whole stack under the cursor
Rotate the card 90° (sideways, not a flip)
Gather the stack under the cursor
Shuffle the stack under the cursor

shortcutsList.mobileNote: On touch devices, long-press a card to open its action bar.

leaveConfirm.title: Exit room?
leaveConfirm.body: You will leave this room and a fresh one opens with a new link, with you as its host. Any cards you owned here are released to the other players.

settings.title: Settings
settings.resetDefaultsHint: Return music, effects and master volume to their recommended levels.

support.title: Support Vaerum
support.intro: Vaerum is built by an independent designer. Your support unlocks the next steps:
lines: Develop the game: new card cycles, balance work, animations, and atmosphere polish.
Physical card edition: custom card stock, embossed sigils, and box production.
3D Steam edition: an animated 3D release with full effects and sound.
support.noUrl: A support page is not configured yet. Please check back later.
support.thanks: Thank you for keeping the table alive.

seal.name: Seal
seal.description: A permanent structure placed on your tableau. Passive power. The path to Ascension.

spell.name: Spell
spell.description: A one-shot attack played from the hand for 1 HP. Resolved, then discarded.

intervention.name: Intervention
intervention.description: A reactive card. Costs no HP and may be played at any moment, even out of turn.

servant.name: Servant
servant.description: A living shield on your tableau. Opponents must clear your Servants before they can target your Seals.

timeRift.name: Time Rift
timeRift.effect: During your Focus phase you draw 3 cards instead of 2. Each additional copy adds +1 draw (max 4 copies, 6 cards). Becomes active from the turn AFTER it is placed.
timeRift.flavor: A hairline crack in the hour, widening with every breath.

veilOfVoid.name: Veil of Void
veilOfVoid.effect: Your other Seals and your hand cannot be targeted by enemy spells or effects. Veil of Void itself remains targetable. Active immediately when placed. Multiple copies do not stack.
veilOfVoid.flavor: Where it falls, the world forgets to look.

crimsonMonolith.name: Crimson Monolith
crimsonMonolith.effect: During your Action phase you spend 3 HP instead of 2. Each additional copy adds +1 HP, but your total HP can never exceed 5. Becomes active from the turn AFTER it is placed.
crimsonMonolith.flavor: A throne of rust and resolve.

necromancersEye.name: Necromancer's Eye
necromancersEye.effect: At the start of your Closing phase, before the hand-limit check, you MUST take the top card of the Discard into your hand. Each additional copy takes one more. If the Discard is empty the effect fizzles. Works on the very turn it is placed.
necromancersEye.flavor: The dead keep their accounts in long, patient ledgers.

etherStrike.name: Ether Strike
etherStrike.effect: Spend 1 HP. Destroy 1 card on a rival's tableau. The Servant Shield rule applies: if the rival has any Servant, you must target a Servant first. If the chosen target is untargetable, the spell cannot be cast, no HP is spent.
etherStrike.flavor: A clean cut between the seen and the sealed.

shadowTheft.name: Shadow Theft
shadowTheft.effect: Spend 1 HP. Choose a rival; their hand is turned face-down and one card is taken at random into your hand. If their hand is empty or shielded by Veil of Void, this spell cannot be cast.
shadowTheft.flavor: Every secret has a weight you can lift.

ancientSight.name: Ancient Sight
ancientSight.effect: Spend 1 HP. Draw 3 cards from the deck. It only ever benefits you; no opponent is targeted. If the deck empties mid-draw, Ether Resonance resolves first, then the remaining draws come from the new deck.
ancientSight.flavor: Open the eye that never blinks.

mindParasite.name: Mind Parasite
mindParasite.effect: Spend 1 HP. Take control of one Servant from a rival's tableau and move it to your own. The stolen Servant counts toward your 3-Servant limit. If you already have 3 Servants or the target has no Servants, this spell cannot be cast, no HP is spent. All ongoing effects transfer to the new owner.
mindParasite.flavor: Loyalty is a door, and you have the key.

twistOfFate.name: Twist of Fate
twistOfFate.effect: Spend 1 HP. Exchange every card in your hand with every card in a chosen rival's hand. Tableau cards are unaffected. Hand-limit excess is resolved by each player at their next Closing phase. Cannot be cast if the target is protected by Veil of Void.

silence.name: Silence!
silence.effect: Costs no HP. Played at any moment, on anyone's turn, to instantly cancel a freshly cast Spell or Intervention. The cancelled card and Silence! both go to the Discard. Silence! can be cancelled by another Silence!, the topmost Silence! cancels the one below.

karmicReflection.name: Karmic Reflection
karmicReflection.effect: Costs no HP. Play it only to cancel an attack aimed at you or at a card you control in play (your Seals and Servants on your tableau). It does not shield your hand or your deck, and it cannot protect another player. The cancelled attack rebounds onto the attacker and resolves against them in full. On the rebound, the attacker's own Servant Shield applies: if they have any Servant in play, the attack must destroy those Servants first and only then can it reach their Seals.
karmicReflection.flavor: What you send returns, in kind.

bloodAtonement.name: Blood Atonement
bloodAtonement.effect: Costs no HP. Played ONLY when one of YOUR own Seals is about to be destroyed. Discard 2 random cards from your hand to save the Seal. If you have fewer than 2 cards in hand, this card cannot be played.

runicWarden.name: Runic Warden
runicWarden.effect: While Runic Warden is on your tableau, ALL of your Seals gain the 'untargetable' status. Runic Warden itself is always targetable. As a normal Servant, the Servant Shield rule applies: rivals must destroy Wardens (and any other Servant) before they can target Seals.

glacialAberration.name: Glacial Aberration
glacialAberration.effect: When this Servant is destroyed, the player who destroyed it skips their entire next turn (Focus, Action, and Closing). Interventions may still be played during the skipped turn. The penalty applies even if the destruction was indirect (Shadow Slayer entry, Karmic Reflection redirect, etc.).
glacialAberration.flavor: A winter that keeps its own time.

shadowSlayer.name: Shadow Slayer
shadowSlayer.effect: When you place Shadow Slayer, if any rival has at least one Servant you MUST choose one of those Servants and destroy it immediately. If a Glacial Aberration is destroyed this way, YOU receive its skip-next-turn penalty. If no rival Servant exists, the entry effect fizzles but Shadow Slayer still enters play.

rulesDoc.title: Vaerum: Heirs of the Ether
rulesDoc.subtitle: The Complete Rulebook
rulesDoc.intro: At the centre of all things lies the Ether, a sourceless force, fractured long ago. Its shards have changed hands through silent treaties, whispered threats, and quiet betrayals. You sit at the table now: a visionary, a schemer, one of the few who can still bend the Ether to a will. There is no friendship here. There is no mercy. There is no luck. Only manipulation, strategy, and the unblinking war for power.

sections.title: 1. Components
body: Vaerum plays with a single deck of 72 cards. Nothing else is required.
Distribution:
Seal (Violet) x16: 4 unique seals, 4 copies each.
Spell (Crimson) x24: 5 unique spells in counts of 2 to 8.
Intervention (Azure) x16: 3 unique cards in counts of 4 to 8.
Servant (Verdant) x16: 3 unique cards in counts of 4 to 8.
Colour key:
SEAL grants passive power and forms the path to victory.
SPELL is a single-use attack; resolved, then discarded.
INTERVENTION costs no HP and may be played at any time.
SERVANT shields your Seals and offers unique abilities.

sections.title: 2. Setup
body: Shuffle the 72-card deck thoroughly.
Deal 5 cards to each player; keep them hidden.
Place the remaining deck face-down at the centre.
Each player reveals one card from the deck; the highest category in the order Servant, Spell, Seal, Intervention chooses the first player. Re-draw on ties.
Play proceeds clockwise.
Player counts: 2 means fast and direct; 3 means balanced and tactical; 4 means the recommended chaotic, diplomatic seat.

sections.title: 3. Zones
body: Each player owns three personal zones; two are shared.
HAND: hidden cards. Rivals see the count, not the contents. Hand limit is 7; excess is discarded at Closing. Seals and Servants do nothing while in the hand.

TABLEAU: face-up zone holding your Seals and Servants. Maximum 4 Seals and 3 Servants. If your tableau is full and you wish to play another, choose one of your existing cards and Discard it first; this does not cost an extra action.
DECK (shared): face-down central pile. When it empties, Ether Resonance is triggered (see section 9).
DISCARD (shared): face-up pile of spent Spells, cancelled cards, destroyed Seals and Servants.

sections.title: 4. Turn flow
body: On your turn, perform the three phases below in this exact order. Phases cannot be skipped or reordered.

PHASE 1, FOCUS: Draw 2 cards from the deck. If it empties mid-draw, Ether Resonance resolves first and the remaining draws come from the new deck. With Time Rift in play, draw 3 (4, 5, 6 with additional copies).
PHASE 2, ACTION: You have 2 HP this turn (3 to 5 if Crimson Monolith is in play, capped at 5). Spend each HP on any single action below, in any combination. Unspent HP is lost; it does not roll over.
CREATE (1 HP): cast 1 Spell from hand, or place 1 Seal or 1 Servant onto your tableau.
STUDY (1 HP): draw 1 extra card from the deck.
CLEANSE (1 HP): discard 1 card from your hand, then draw 1 card. The card drawn by Cleanse cannot be discarded by another Cleanse in the same turn.

PHASE 3, CLOSING: First, every active Seal trigger fires (Necromancer's Eye takes its card from the Discard now). Then check the hand limit; discard down to 7 if needed. After Closing is complete, if you have at least 3 Seals on your tableau you MAY declare Ascension (see section 10).

sections.title: 5. Card-type rules
body: SEALS are permanent. They do nothing while in your hand. Their effects activate only on your tableau.
SPELLS cost 1 HP. You must declare a legal target before casting. If every potential target is untargetable, the spell cannot be cast and HP is not spent.
INTERVENTIONS cost no HP. They may be played at any moment, on anyone's turn, as a reaction to the most recent action.
Karmic Reflection and Blood Atonement are SELFISH: usable only to protect YOURSELF or your own cards.

Silence! is UNIVERSAL: usable to cancel any newly cast Spell or Intervention, regardless of target. Placement (Create) of a Seal or Servant is NOT a Spell or Intervention, so Silence! cannot stop a tableau placement.

SERVANTS: the SERVANT SHIELD is absolute. While a player has at least one Servant on their tableau, no rival may target any of that player's Seals directly. Even targetable cards like Runic Warden cannot be skipped; the rival must clear every Servant first.

sections.title: 6. Chained reactions
body: When an action is taken, any player may add an Intervention to the chain in response. The chain stays open until everyone passes; it then resolves from top to bottom, the most recent card resolves first.

Example: A casts Ether Strike (bottom). B answers with Silence! (middle). A answers with another Silence! (top). Resolution: A's Silence! cancels B's Silence!; both go to Discard. The bottom Ether Strike resolves normally.

Silence! interactions: a cancelled card and the Silence! that cancelled it both go to the Discard. The HP of a cancelled spell is NOT refunded. Karmic Reflection can be cancelled by Silence!; in that case the reflection never happens and the original attack hits its target.

sections.title: 7. Untargetable status
body: Some effects make their subject immune to enemy actions. Untargetability is layered: each independent layer must be removed individually.
Veil of Void: your other Seals and your hand are untargetable by enemy spells and effects. Veil of Void itself is always targetable.

Runic Warden: while on your tableau, all of your Seals are untargetable. Runic Warden is a Servant and obeys the Servant Shield rule; rivals must still destroy all your Servants before targeting your Seals.

Stacking: if both Veil of Void and Runic Warden are in play, your Seals are protected by two separate layers. In practice the rival must clear every Servant (including Runic Warden) and then destroy Veil of Void before reaching the Seals beneath.

sections.title: 8. Seal stacking
body: Multiple copies of the same Seal can sit on your tableau. New copies activate starting from the turn after they are placed (Necromancer's Eye is the lone exception, it activates the same turn).
Time Rift: stacks. Each copy adds +1 to Focus draws. (1=3, 2=4, 3=5, 4=6.)
Crimson Monolith: stacks, capped. Each copy adds +1 HP up to a hard cap of 5 total HP.
Necromancer's Eye: stacks. Each copy takes one more card from the Discard at your Closing.
Veil of Void: does NOT stack. Extra copies provide no extra benefit.

sections.title: 9. Ether Resonance
body: When the central deck is fully exhausted, play freezes. The Discard is taken, shuffled, and becomes the new deck. This shuffle is the one and only Ether Resonance event of that cycle.
Sacrifice order: every player loses one card in the following priority:
First, the tableau: choose any one Seal or Servant on your tableau and send it to the Discard. The choice is yours.
Then, the hand: ONLY if your tableau is completely empty does the resonance reach your hand. Discard 1 random card.
If a draw effect was mid-resolution when the deck emptied, resonance resolves fully first; remaining draws come from the new deck.
If both the deck and the Discard are empty, Resonance does not trigger and no new deck is formed. Effects that require a draw simply fail to draw for this attempt; play continues.

sections.title: 10. Victory, Ascension
body: DECLARATION: at the end of your Closing phase, if you have at least 3 Seals on your tableau, you MAY declare Ascension. Declaration is irrevocable.

TRIAL: every other player gets one full turn before the turn returns to you. During the Trial, anyone may use Spells, Interventions, and Servants to destroy, steal, or disable your Seals. Interventions are unrestricted.
VICTORY: when the turn returns to you, BEFORE Focus begins, if you still have at least 3 Seals intact on your tableau, you win the game.
Only one active Ascension exists at a time. Other players must wait for it to resolve (in success or failure) before they can declare their own.
Reaching a third Seal during the Trial does NOT grant a new declaration; Ascension is declared only at the end of your own turn.
If you lose Seals during the Trial and have fewer than 3 when the turn returns, the Ascension fails and play continues normally.

sections.title: 11. Card encyclopedia
body: Every card resolves exactly as written on its face. When a card text and a general rule appear to disagree, the card text wins.
SEALS (16 total, 4 unique × 4 copies):
Time Rift: During your Focus phase you draw 3 cards instead of 2. Each additional copy adds +1 draw (max 4 copies, 6 cards). Becomes active from the turn AFTER it is placed.
Veil of Void: Your other Seals and your hand cannot be targeted by enemy spells or effects. Veil of Void itself remains targetable. Active immediately when placed. Multiple copies do not stack.
Crimson Monolith: During your Action phase you spend 3 HP instead of 2. Each additional copy adds +1 HP, but your total HP can never exceed 5. Becomes active from the turn AFTER it is placed.

Necromancer's Eye: At the start of your Closing phase, before the hand-limit check, you MUST take the top card of the Discard into your hand. Each additional copy takes one more. If the Discard is empty the effect fizzles. Works on the very turn it is placed.
SPELLS (24 total):
Ether Strike: 1 HP. Destroy 1 card on a rival's tableau. Servant Shield applies.
Shadow Theft: 1 HP. Choose a rival; their hand is turned face-down and 1 card is taken at random into your hand. Blocked by Veil of Void.
Ancient Sight: 1 HP. Draw 3 cards from the deck. Self-targeted only; no opponent involved.
Mind Parasite: 1 HP. Take control of one Servant from a rival's tableau. Counts toward your 3-Servant limit.
Twist of Fate: 1 HP. Swap every card in your hand with every card in a chosen rival's hand. Blocked by Veil of Void.
INTERVENTIONS (16 total):
Silence!: 0 HP. Cancel a freshly cast Spell or Intervention. Both cards go to the Discard. Universal target.
Karmic Reflection: 0 HP. Selfish. Cancels an attack aimed at you or your cards in play and rebounds it onto the attacker, where the attacker's own Servant Shield applies.
Blood Atonement: 0 HP. Selfish. When one of your Seals is about to be destroyed, discard 2 random cards from hand to save it.
SERVANTS (16 total):
Runic Warden: While in play, ALL of your Seals gain untargetable. Servant Shield still applies, so rivals must clear your Wardens first.
Glacial Aberration: When destroyed, the destroyer skips their entire next turn (Focus, Action, Closing). Interventions still allowed during the skip.
Shadow Slayer: On entry, if any rival has a Servant you MUST destroy one. If it kills a Glacial Aberration this way, YOU receive the skip penalty.

sections.title: 12. Edge-case guide
body: Q. What happens to a Spell cancelled by Silence!? A. The cancelled Spell and the Silence! both go to the Discard. The caster's 1 HP is not refunded.
Q. Can Time Rift placed this turn give me extra cards? A. No. Its effect starts on your next turn, Focus has already happened this turn.
Q. Two Time Rifts, how many cards? A. 4 (each copy adds +1).
Q. Crimson Monolith placed this turn, does it give me 3 HP now? A. No. It activates next turn.
Q. Necromancer's Eye placed this turn, does it fire at this Closing? A. Yes. Its trigger is during Closing, which is after Action.
Q. Shadow Slayer enters but no rival has Servants? A. The entry effect fizzles. Shadow Slayer still enters as a normal Servant.
Q. Shadow Slayer kills a Glacial Aberration on entry, who skips? A. You do. The destroyer pays the penalty.

Q. When Karmic Reflection rebounds an attack, does the attacker's Servant Shield apply? A. Yes. The rebounding attack must respect the attacker's Servant Shield, so it has to clear their Servants before it can reach their Seals.
Q. Blood Atonement needs how many cards in hand? A. At least 2. With fewer, it cannot be played.
Q. Mind Parasite when your tableau already has 3 Servants? A. Cannot be cast; no HP spent.
Q. Shadow Theft against a hand protected by Veil of Void? A. Not legal; cannot be cast.
Q. Twist of Fate against Veil of Void? A. Not legal; the targeted player's hand is shielded.
Q. Can a player skipped by Glacial Aberration still play Interventions? A. Yes. Interventions are reactive and ignore turn order.
Q. The deck empties mid-draw? A. Ether Resonance resolves fully first; the draw effect then completes from the new deck.

sections.title: 13. Quick reference
body: Phases: Focus, Action, Closing, (optional) Ascension declaration.
HP: 0 HP for Interventions; 1 HP per Create, Study, or Cleanse; 2 HP base; up to 5 HP with Crimson Monolith.
Limits: Hand 7, Seals 4, Servants 3, HP cap 5.
Card totals: 16 Seals + 24 Spells + 16 Interventions + 16 Servants = 72.
Closing order: Seal triggers first, then hand-limit check.

sections.title: 14. The digital table
body: Vaerum on the web is a sandbox. There is no automated rules engine; players follow this rulebook themselves.

Left-press drags a card. Right-click flips the stack under the cursor. Ctrl plus left-press drags the whole stack. Scroll flips a single card; Ctrl plus scroll flips the whole stack. Shift plus scroll rotates the card 90° sideways. Press G to gather the stack, M to shuffle it.

Your zone is the blurred strip along the bottom of your screen. Cards there are private to you; rivals see only their backs and can infer the count. You cannot drop a card inside an opponent's zone; leave it on the rim and the owner draws it in.
When everyone leaves, the room closes. Leaving the room opens a fresh one with a new link, with you as its host.

rulesDoc.closing: Show no mercy at the table.

resetDeckConfirm.title: Reset deck?
resetDeckConfirm.body: Every card on the table is gathered, shuffled face-down and returned to the deck. This affects all players.
